title: PL Stock Just Jumped 10% on a Greek Satellite Deal — and Wall Street Hasn't Caught Up Yet
text:
Planet Labs stock surged more than 10% following a significant two-year satellite imagery contract with the Greek government, supported by the European Space Agency. The German operating division of Planet Labs negotiated this seven-figure contract for satellite imagery services. This represents a major milestone for the company's expanding presence in European defense and government sectors.

The deal showcases Planet Labs' growing appeal to government agencies seeking Earth observation capabilities. The contract adds to the company's impressive backlog of over $900 million, predominantly from defense and government customers. With recurring revenue representing more than 90% of total revenue and gross margins expanding to 58%, Planet Labs continues to demonstrate strong business fundamentals.

This European success follows other major contract wins, including a €240 million multi-year German government agreement and a multi-year, low nine-figure agreement with Sweden's Armed Forces. The company's fiscal 2026 results showed record quarterly revenue of $86.8 million, up 41% year-over-year, marking its first full year of non-GAAP profitability with adjusted EBITDA of $15.5 million.
